title: betula mandshurica var. rockii
intro: Betula mandshurica var. rockii, also known as Korean paper birch, is a deciduous tree species native to the mountainous regions of Korea and China. The bark of the Korean paper birch has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Betula mandshurica var. rockii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs and sore throats, and act as an expectorant to relieve bronchitis and other respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Korean paper birch include tea infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits and promote overall well-being.
